text:
Once upon a time, there was a little rabbit named Flop. Flop lived in a burrow filled with toys and a cozy spot under the burrow’s entrance. Life was simple for Flop—eating clover, enjoying sunny days, and occasionally getting chased by bigger rabbits who wanted Flop’s carrots.

One sunny morning, Mr. McGregor, a kindly old sheep who sold fresh vegetables to the village, appeared at Flop’s burrow. He had big bags of carrots and told Flop that they were the best carrots in town.

Flop couldn’t resist! He scampered out of his burrow, skipping through the meadows and past the wild berry bushes. Just as he reached the orchard where Mr. McGregor was standing, he accidentally kicked a bucket of water onto McGregor. Instead of vegetables, a cold, wet splash flew through the air and drenched Flop.

The shock of the cold water started Flop shaking, and in his haste, he forgot about
